title_variants: Victorian Annex with Garden & Free Parking
Stylish Lodge Near Peak District, Private Road
Cozy 4‑guest Retreat, Gardens, Smart TV, Free Parking
analysis.rewritten_desc:
Relax in style at The Little Lodge – a brand‑new, fully renovated annex attached to a charming 1800s Victorian lodge. Set back on a private, tree‑lined road in the leafy Ranmoor suburb of Sheffield, the property is surrounded by mature gardens, wildlife and peaceful conservation land. Just 15 minutes from Sheffield’s main train station and on the foothills of the Peak District, it’s an ideal base for city breaks or rambling getaways. 

Inside, the ground‑floor open‑plan living area blends a comfortable sofa that folds into a double bed with a fully equipped kitchen featuring an electric oven, hob, fridge, SMEG kettle, toaster and coffee machine. A SMART TV with an Amazon Fire Stick gives instant access to all your favourite streaming apps. The upstairs main bedroom offers a European‑size double bed (between a double and king) and a private en‑suite bathroom with a large shower. Downstairs you’ll find a stylish, jazzy bathroom for guests. 

All essentials are provided: fresh linens, extra pillows, towels, hair‑dryer, iron, ironing board, cleaning products, and a full set of toiletries. Outdoor enthusiasts can enjoy the private garden and optional garden furniture, while free on‑site parking makes arrivals hassle‑free. An iron, hanging rack and clothes storage keep the space tidy, and a travel cot, high‑chair and baby bath are available on request. Your whole stay is yours to enjoy – from sunrise in the garden to a cozy night in the lodge.
analysis.photo_order_rec: Use a bright garden‑view exterior shot as the cover photo – it highlights the private road, green space and free parking, the top three search keywords guests look for.
key_diagnosis: Reduce the nightly rate from £146.8 to closer to the market median (£104.9) – you’re charging ~40% more while occupancy is only 52.2%, just 3.3% above the cohort median.
Add a high‑value amenity such as air‑conditioning or a tumble dryer to justify the premium price and attract guests seeking extra comfort.
Increase the photo count from 17 to at least the market median of 23 and reorder to lead with a garden exterior image to improve visual appeal and click‑through rates.
analysis.outreach_hook: Your rate of £146.8/night is about 40% higher than the local median of £104.9, yet your 90‑day occupancy is only 52.2%, just 3% above market average.
